title: Gas stoves: New York will vote on first statewide law banning natural gas in new construction
text: New York may soon become the first state in the nation to ban natural gas for stoves and other appliances in new construction when lawmakers vote on a budget plan next week.

title: Consumer goods stocks are set to keep running, and it's not too late to buy, Jim Cramer says
text: Food and consumer goods stocks are poised to keep running, Jim Cramer said Friday, at least if no external news drags down the entire market.

title: Colgate (CL) Q1 Earnings & Sales Beat Estimates, View Up
text: On an organic basis, Colgate-Palmolive's (CL) first-quarter 2023 sales advance 10%, with improvements in all divisions and categories.

title: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
text: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.73 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.70 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.74 per share a year ago.

title: Colgate-Palmolive beats consensus in 1Q after price hikes
text: Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) has reported a stronger-than-expected first quarter after it pushed through double-digit price increases.  The consumer goods company reported an 8.5% increase in sales to $4.77 billion for the three months to March 31, 2023, beating the $4.59 billion expected by Wall Street.

title: 3 Top Earnings Acceleration Stocks Worth Buying
text: Notable companies to have witnessed solid earnings acceleration as of now are ColgatePalmolive (CL), BigCommerce (BIGC) & Exact Sciences (EXAS).

title: Colgate-Palmolive's stock gains premarket as earnings top estimates and company offers upbeat guidance
text: Colgate-Palmolive Co.'s stock CL rose 1.4% in premarket trade Friday, after the consumer goods company posted better-than-expected first-quarter earnings. The New York-based company posted net income of $372 million, or 45 cents a share, for the quarter, down from $559 million, or 66 cents a share, in the year-earlier period.

title: Colgate Announces 1st Quarter 2023 Results
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL): Net sales increased 8.5%; Organic sales* increased 10.0% with growth in every division and in all four categories GAAP EPS declined 32% to $0.45; Base Business EPS* declined 1% to $0.73 GAAP Gross profit margin and Base Business Gross profit margin* both decreased 160 basis points to 56.9%, including a negative 90 basis point impact from private label sales resulting from the previously disclosed acquisitions of pet food businesses.

title: Here's How Colgate (CL) is Poised Ahead of Q1 Earnings
text: Colgate's (CL) first-quarter 2023 sales are likely to have gained from innovation, brand strength, focus on premiumization and expansion efforts.

title: Colgate-Palmolive Webcasts 2023 First Quarter Earnings Conference Call April 28, 2023 – 8:30 a.m. ET

The call will be hosted by Chairman, President and CEO, Noel Wallace, Chief Financial Officer, Stan Sutula, and Chief Investor Relations Officer and SVP, M&A, John Faucher. title: My Dividend Stock Portfolio: New All-Time Dividend Record - 99 Holdings With 25 Buys
text: Inflation and (expected) actions by the Federal Reserve continue to dominate market movements even though the turmoil in the financial sector shortly took center stage. title: 7 Best Stocks to Buy as Inflation Fears Creep Up
text: While 2022 represented the year that saw inflation spike higher, the subsequent monetary tightening actions by the Federal Reserve cooled demand for the best stocks for inflation. However, a surprise threat from abroad – namely, the oil production cuts by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and non-member oil-producing nations – risk sending prices northward once again.

title: Here's Why Investors Should Hold Colgate (CL) Stock for Now
text: Pricing actions, revenue management initiatives, innovation, brand strength and digital growth position Colgate (CL) for growth amid higher costs and currency headwinds.

title: Looking for Yield and Dividend Growth? This Consumer Staples Dividend King Outshines Its Peers
text: Investors looking for reliable dividend stocks often start with Dividend Kings, which have over five decades of annual dividend increases behind them. In the consumer staples space, Procter & Gamble, Kimberly-Clark, and Colgate-Palmolive all make the list.

title: Johnson & Johnson, Procter & Gamble Among 11 Companies To Continue Dividend Growth In Early April
text: Eleven companies announced annual dividend increases in March. Colgate-Palmolive started its 7th decade of dividend growth in March with a 2% boost. Steel Dynamics followed up last year's 31% boost with a 25% increase, resulting in a 63% payout increase since 2022.
